title: Bioschemas Life Sciences Training — Complete Schema
description: A comprehensive JSON Schema covering all three Bioschemas training profiles: TrainingMaterial (v1.1-DRAFT), Course (v1.0-RELEASE), and CourseInstance (v1.0-RELEASE). Each profile definition includes full property descriptions, marginality annotations (Minimum/Recommended/Optional), cardinality, controlled-vocabulary guidance, TeSS-ingestion notes, and concrete examples. Use this schema both for validation of JSON-LD payloads and as authoritative documentation when generating Bioschemas markup.
$comment: Sources: bioschemas.org profile pages for TrainingMaterial/1.1-DRAFT, Course/1.0-RELEASE, CourseInstance/1.0-RELEASE; TeSS RDF extractor documentation. Best-practice summary: (1) Always include @context, @type, @id, name, description, keywords, dct:conformsTo. (2) Use ORCID URIs in author/@id. (3) Use ontology URIs in about[].url — EDAM (http://edamontology.org/) is the primary ontology for life-science topics, but other domain ontologies are equally valid: PaNET (https://pan-ontologies.github.io/PaNET/) covers photon/neutron and large-scale facility science, and further ontologies (e.g. NCI Thesaurus, MeSH, OBI) may be used for their respective domains. The llm-metadata-generator is designed to support multiple configurable ontologies — see TODO.md item 6. (4) Use DOIs in identifier. (5) Use ISO 8601 for all dates and durations. (6) Use SPDX short identifiers or CC-BY URLs for license. (7) Use IETF BCP 47 language codes (e.g. 'en', 'en-GB') for inLanguage. (8) Link Course to its CourseInstance(s) via hasCourseInstance.

examples.name: Introduction to Bioinformatics
examples.description: A short guide to basic bioinformatics concepts.

examples.name: Applied Genomics
examples.description: A semester-long module on applied genomics.

Context.title: @context
Context.description: Used to provide the context (namespaces) for the JSON-LD file. Not needed in other serialisations. Marginality: Minimum.
Context.$comment: Best practice: use {'@vocab': 'https://schema.org/', 'dct': 'http://purl.org/dc/terms/'} to support both schema.org properties and the Dublin Core dct:conformsTo declaration required by Bioschemas.

ConformsTo.title: dct:conformsTo
ConformsTo.description: Used to state the Bioschemas profile that the markup relates to. The versioned URL of the profile must be used. Use the full Dublin Core URI http://purl.org/dc/terms/conformsTo as the property name in markup. Marginality: Minimum.
ConformsTo.$comment:
Best practice: always include dct:conformsTo so that TeSS and other ingestors can determine which Bioschemas profile the resource conforms to and apply the correct extraction logic. Use the object form {'@id': 'https://bioschemas.org/profiles/X/Y', '@type': 'CreativeWork'} for maximum compatibility. TeSS uses this value to select the correct extractor and for validation reporting.

Comprehensive list of Bioschemas training-related profile versions (use the most recent release for new markup):

Person.title: Person
Person.description: A person, typically an author, instructor, contributor, or organizer.
Person.$comment: Best practice: always include @id with the ORCID URI (https://orcid.org/...) when the ORCID is known. TeSS extracts ORCID from @id by regex. Use identifier for legacy ORCID placement. The @type field should be 'Person'.

@id.description: ORCID URI or other persistent identifier for this person.
@id.$comment: Best practice: use ORCID URI (https://orcid.org/XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX). TeSS extracts ORCID from @id by regex pattern matching.

identifier.description: ORCID or other identifier (legacy location; prefer @id).

Organization.title: Organization
Organization.description: An organization, typically a provider, publisher, funder, or organizer.

Agent.title: Person or Organization
Agent.description: A person or organization involved in creating, contributing to, or delivering the resource.
Agent.$comment: TeSS uses extract_people() to parse agent nodes. ORCID is detected by regex from @id or identifier. For arrays, supply one object per person/organisation.

oneOf.$comment: Plain strings are supported by TeSS but do not allow ORCID or organisation URI attachment. Use structured objects where possible.

AgentOrArray.title: Person, Organization, or array thereof
AgentOrArray.description: One or more persons or organizations.

DefinedTerm.title: DefinedTerm
DefinedTerm.description: A term from a controlled vocabulary, used to describe topics, skills, or learning objectives. Multiple ontologies are supported; choose the most appropriate for your domain.
DefinedTerm.$comment:
Several ontologies can be used for about[], teaches, and related properties. Choose the ontology most appropriate for the resource's domain:

- EDAM (http://edamontology.org/): the primary ontology for life-science / bioinformatics topics and operations. Use topic_XXXX for subject areas and operation_XXXX for methods. TeSS captures EDAM URIs specially into scientific_topic_uris for faceted browsing. Browse at https://edamontology.github.io/edam-browser/ .

- PaNET (https://pan-ontologies.github.io/PaNET/): covers photon, neutron, and large-scale research-facility (synchrotron, free-electron laser, neutron source) training topics. Used by PaN training platforms. Browse at https://pan-ontologies.github.io/PaNET/index-en.html .

- NCI Thesaurus (http://ncicb.nci.nih.gov/xml/owl/EVS/Thesaurus.owl#): broad biomedical and clinical vocabulary.

- MeSH (https://id.nlm.nih.gov/mesh/): NLM Medical Subject Headings — useful for clinical/health topics.

- OBI (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/): Ontology for Biomedical Investigations.

The llm-metadata-generator is designed to support multiple configurable ontologies — see TODO.md item 6. The 'name' field should match the canonical label in the chosen ontology.

name.description: Human-readable label for the term, matching the canonical label in the source ontology.

url.description: URI of the term in its defining ontology. Use EDAM URIs for life-science topics, PaNET URIs for photon/neutron science topics, or terms from other domain-appropriate ontologies.

@id.description: Alternative location for the ontology term URI.

ThingOrArray.title: Thing, DefinedTerm, URI, or array thereof
ThingOrArray.description: A subject, topic, skill, or learning outcome. Can be a DefinedTerm with ontology URI, a plain string, or a URI. MANY cardinality: use an array when multiple values apply.
ThingOrArray.$comment: Best practice: use DefinedTerm with ontology URIs for machine-readable topic tagging. Use EDAM URIs (http://edamontology.org/) for life-science topics — TeSS captures these as scientific_topic_uris for faceted browsing. Use PaNET URIs (https://pan-ontologies.github.io/PaNET/) for photon/neutron science topics. Plain text strings are also accepted and stored as free-text tags.

TextOrArray.title: Text or array of Text
TextOrArray.description: A text value or array of text values.

Identifier.title: Identifier
Identifier.description: An identifier for the resource. Can be a DOI URL, a plain string, or a PropertyValue object.
Identifier.$comment: Best practice: use a full DOI URL (https://doi.org/10.XXXX/...) when available. TeSS detects DOI patterns by regex (10.\d+/...) and stores them in the :doi field for special treatment. The PropertyValue form is also supported with propertyID='doi' and value='10.XXXX/...'.

oneOf.description: DOI URL or other identifier string.

AlignmentObject.title: AlignmentObject
AlignmentObject.description: An alignment object describing a prerequisite competency or educational alignment using a structured reference.
AlignmentObject.$comment: Used in coursePrerequisites and competencyRequired. alignmentType should be 'requires' or 'educationalLevel'. targetUrl should point to a term in a competency framework.

educationalFramework.description: The educational framework the alignment is defined within.

targetUrl.description: URL of the target alignment in its framework.

examples.targetName: Higher Education Credit Framework For England

License.title: License
License.description: A license document that applies to this content. Use a CC-BY URL or SPDX short identifier (e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0') when possible.
License.$comment: Best practice: use a canonical Creative Commons URL (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/) or an SPDX short identifier string. Avoid the sentinel value 'notspecified' — TeSS drops this value during ingestion. TeSS resolves license values via LicenceDictionary. A missing or 'notspecified' license reduces discoverability and reuse.

PostalAddress.title: PostalAddress
PostalAddress.description: A postal address for a physical location.

addressCountry.$comment: Best practice: use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 two-letter country codes (e.g. 'GB', 'DE', 'US') for interoperability.

Place.title: Place
Place.description: A physical place where the event is held.

VirtualLocation.title: VirtualLocation
VirtualLocation.description: A virtual location for an online event, with a connection URL.
VirtualLocation.$comment: Use VirtualLocation when courseMode contains 'online'. TeSS merges VirtualLocation into the venue text field and sets :online true. Always include a url for participants to join.

name.description: Display name for the virtual venue.

url.description: Connection URL for the virtual location.

Location.title: location
Location.description: The location of the event. Use a Place with PostalAddress for in-person events; a VirtualLocation for online events. Both may be supplied for hybrid events.
Location.$comment: TeSS extract_location() merges Place, PostalAddress and VirtualLocation fields. For hybrid events, supply both a Place and a VirtualLocation. TeSS sets :online=true when a VirtualLocation is present. Use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes for addressCountry.

Offer.title: Offer
Offer.description: Pricing information for the course instance.
Offer.$comment: Use ISO 4217 currency codes for priceCurrency (e.g. 'GBP', 'EUR', 'USD'). Use ISO 8601 date for validFrom/validThrough.

price.description: Price as a string, e.g. '0.00' for free or '100.00'.

url.description: URL where registration or payment occurs.

Mention.title: Mention
Mention.description: A tool, dataset, or other resource referenced or actively used in this training.
Mention.$comment: TeSS maps mentions to :external_resources as {title:, url:} objects. Use @type 'SoftwareApplication' or a bio.tools URL for software tools, 'Dataset' for datasets. Include url for discoverability.

@type.description: Schema.org type of the mentioned resource.

examples.name: Galaxy
examples.description: Open, web-based platform for data intensive biomedical research

TrainingMaterial.title: TrainingMaterial / LearningResource
TrainingMaterial.description: A Bioschemas TrainingMaterial (v1.1-DRAFT) describes bioscience training material on the Web. Schema.org type: LearningResource (Thing > CreativeWork > LearningResource). A training material is a single document, slide deck, video, or dataset used for educational purposes. It differs from Course (which describes a recurring programme) and CourseInstance (a specific run). Profile URL: https://bioschemas.org/profiles/TrainingMaterial/1.1-DRAFT
TrainingMaterial.$comment: Minimum required: @context, @type, @id, dct:conformsTo, name, description, keywords. Strongly recommended: url, author (with ORCID @id), license (CC URL or SPDX), about (EDAM DefinedTerm), teaches, learningResourceType, inLanguage, educationalLevel, identifier (DOI). TeSS extracts: name->:title, description, keywords, author, contributor, license, inLanguage, learningResourceType, teaches, about (EDAM), identifier (DOI), version, dateCreated/Modified/Published, educationalLevel, competencyRequired, mentions, url.

@type.title: @type
@type.description: Schema.org/Bioschemas class. Use 'LearningResource' (preferred), 'TrainingMaterial', or 'CreativeWork'. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: MANY.
@type.$comment: TeSS queries for RDF::Vocab::SCHEMA.LearningResource to find training materials. 'TrainingMaterial' is a Bioschemas extension type. Both are recognised.

@id.title: @id
@id.description: Globally unique IRI identifying this training material. Should be a stable, persistent URI (e.g. DOI URL or canonical page URL). Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.
@id.$comment: Best practice: use the canonical URL of the resource page, or a DOI URL. TeSS uses @id as a fallback for :url when url is absent.

name.title: name
name.description: The name / title of the training material. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.
name.$comment: TeSS maps name to :title. Keep the title concise and descriptive.

description.title: description
description.description: A description of the training material. May contain plain text or HTML (TeSS converts HTML to Markdown during ingestion). Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.
description.$comment: TeSS normalises descriptions via convert_description (HTML-to-Markdown). Include enough detail for discoverability. Avoid very short single-sentence descriptions.
examples: A comprehensive tutorial covering the fundamentals of RNA-Seq analysis using standard bioinformatics tools.

keywords.title: keywords
keywords.description: Keywords or tags describing the content. May be a comma-separated string or an array. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: MANY.
keywords.$comment: TeSS splits comma-separated strings using extract_keyword_like. Best practice: prefer an array of strings. Use controlled vocabulary terms where possible. EDAM topic names make good keywords.

url.title: url
url.description: The preferred URL of the training material. You must provide this value if it is known. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
url.$comment: TeSS uses url as the canonical resource URL. If absent, TeSS may fall back to @id. Providing a stable URL is essential for deduplication across scrapes.

about.title: about
about.description: The subject of this training material. Use DefinedTerm with EDAM ontology URIs for machine-readable topic tagging. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
about.$comment: Best practice: use EDAM topic URIs (http://edamontology.org/topic_XXXX). TeSS captures these as :scientific_topic_uris for faceted browsing. Plain text strings are also stored but not linked to ontology terms.

abstract.title: abstract
abstract.description: A short abstract summarising the training material. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
examples: This tutorial introduces the RNA-Seq analysis workflow from raw reads to differential expression.

audience.title: audience
audience.description: A succinct description of the intended target audience, e.g., graduates, postgraduates, clinicians. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
audience.$comment: TeSS maps to :target_audience. Use plain strings (e.g. 'PhD students', 'Bioinformaticians', 'Wet-lab researchers') or Audience objects.

author.title: author
author.description: Those involved in the preparation, creation and/or presentation of the published work, specifically writing the initial draft. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
author.$comment: Best practice: supply structured Person objects with @id set to the ORCID URI. TeSS uses extract_people() to parse ORCID from @id. Include all principal authors; use contributor for secondary contributors.

competencyRequired.title: competencyRequired
competencyRequired.description: Knowledge, skill, ability or personal attribute that must be demonstrated in order to understand this learning resource. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
competencyRequired.$comment: TeSS normalises to strings or markdown-formatted lists via markdownify_list. Can be a plain string, array of strings, or AlignmentObject.

educationalLevel.title: educationalLevel
educationalLevel.description: The students' level of ability in the topic being taught. Use 'beginner', 'intermediate', or 'advanced'. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
educationalLevel.$comment: Best practice: use one of the three standard values — 'beginner', 'intermediate', 'advanced'. TeSS stores this as :difficulty_level and omits the field in output when value is 'notspecified'. Providing this field improves searchability.

identifier.title: identifier
identifier.description: An identifier for this resource such as a DOI. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
identifier.$comment: Best practice: use a full DOI URL (https://doi.org/10.XXXX/...). TeSS detects DOI patterns by regex and stores them separately. Use PropertyValue for structured identifier forms.

inLanguage.title: inLanguage
inLanguage.description: The language of the training material. Defaults to English if not specified. Use IETF BCP 47 language codes. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
inLanguage.$comment: Best practice: use IETF BCP 47 subtags, e.g. 'en', 'en-GB', 'de', 'fr'. TeSS extract_language() returns the first subtag before '-'. For multilingual resources, supply an array.

learningResourceType.title: learningResourceType
learningResourceType.description: The type/kind characterising the learning resource, e.g. 'Tutorial', 'Slides', 'Video', 'e-Learning module'. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
learningResourceType.$comment: TeSS maps to :resource_type. Use recognised values for interoperability. Commonly used values: 'Tutorial', 'Slides', 'Video', 'e-Learning module', 'Script', 'Workflow', 'Vignette', 'Reference guide'.

license.title: license
license.description: The license under which the training material is published. If there is a licence it must be added. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
license.$comment: Best practice: use a canonical CC URL or SPDX identifier. Avoid 'notspecified' — TeSS drops this sentinel. Open licensing (CC-BY or CC0) is strongly encouraged for FAIR reuse.

mentions.title: mentions
mentions.description: Datasets, tools, technologies, or entities referred to or actively used in this training material. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
mentions.$comment: TeSS maps to :external_resources as {title:, url:} objects. Use @type 'SoftwareApplication' for software tools with bio.tools URLs. Include description for context.

sameAs.title: sameAs
sameAs.description: URL of a reference Web page that unambiguously indicates the item's identity, e.g. a Wikipedia page, Wikidata entry, or bio.tools entry. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.

teaches.title: teaches
teaches.description: The competencies or learning outcomes that this training material is intended to teach. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
teaches.$comment: TeSS normalises via markdownify_list. Can be plain strings or DefinedTerm objects. Best practice: phrase as learner-centred outcomes starting with action verbs (e.g. 'Understand de novo assembly', 'Run BLAST searches').

timeRequired.title: timeRequired
timeRequired.description: The estimated time it takes to work through this resource. Use ISO 8601 duration format (e.g. 'PT30M', 'PT1H30M', 'P1D'). Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
timeRequired.$comment: Best practice: use ISO 8601 duration strings. Examples: 'PT30M' = 30 minutes, 'PT1H30M' = 1.5 hours, 'P1D' = 1 day, 'P3D' = 3 days.

accessibilitySummary.title: accessibilitySummary
accessibilitySummary.description: A human-readable summary of specific accessibility features or deficiencies of this material. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.
examples: Subtitles available in English and French.

contributor.title: contributor
contributor.description: Contributors who made non-authorship contributions, e.g., critical review, commentary, or revision. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

creativeWorkStatus.title: creativeWorkStatus
creativeWorkStatus.description: The lifecycle status of this training material. Use 'Active', 'Under development', or 'Archived'. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

dateCreated.title: dateCreated
dateCreated.description: The date on which the training material was created. Use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

dateModified.title: dateModified
dateModified.description: The date on which the training material was most recently modified. Use ISO 8601 format. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

datePublished.title: datePublished
datePublished.description: The date the training material was first published. Use ISO 8601 format. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

hasPart.title: hasPart
hasPart.description: A sub-training material or externally referenced training material that is part of this resource. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

isPartOf.title: isPartOf
isPartOf.description: The Course or other training material that this training material is a part of. Use to reference the containing Course. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

recordedAt.title: recordedAt
recordedAt.description: The CourseInstance or event where this training material was or will be featured. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

version.title: version
version.description: The version of this training material. Strongly recommended if versioned. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

workTranslation.title: workTranslation
workTranslation.description: A work that is a translation of this training material into another language. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

examples.name: Introduction to Bioinformatics
examples.description: A short guide to basic bioinformatics concepts covering sequence analysis, BLAST and genome browsers.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: A Bioschemas Course (v1.0-RELEASE) describes the broad, common aspects of a recurring training programme. Schema.org type: Course (Thing > CreativeWork > Course). A Course captures stable information valid across all runs — use CourseInstance for location/time-specific details. Must be used in tandem with CourseInstance. Profile URL: https://bioschemas.org/profiles/Course/1.0-RELEASE
Course.$comment: Minimum required: @context, @type, @id, dct:conformsTo, name, description, keywords. Strongly recommended: url, about (EDAM), educationalLevel, hasCourseInstance (with at least one CourseInstance), license, provider (Organisation with @id), teaches, timeRequired. TeSS merges Course-level metadata with CourseInstance metadata. hasCourseInstance is output as an inlined CourseInstance in TeSS exports.

@type.title: @type
@type.description: Schema.org/Bioschemas class. Must be 'Course'. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: MANY.

@id.title: @id
@id.description: Globally unique IRI identifying this course. Use a persistent URI such as the canonical page URL. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.

name.title: name
name.description: The name/title of the course. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.

description.title: description
description.description: A description of the course. May be plain text or HTML. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.
examples: This course introduces applied genomics techniques for life scientists.

keywords.title: keywords
keywords.description: Free text keywords delimited by commas describing the Course content. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.
keywords.$comment: Best practice: prefer an array. Use EDAM topic labels as keywords for cross-referencing.

url.title: url
url.description: The preferred URL of the course page. You must provide this value if it is known. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.

about.title: about
about.description: The subject of this Course. Use DefinedTerm with EDAM Topic URIs for machine-readable topic tagging. Controlled vocabulary: EDAM term. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
about.$comment: Best practice: use EDAM topic URIs. TeSS captures these as scientific_topic_uris for faceted browsing.

abstract.title: abstract
abstract.description: A short abstract summarising the course. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.

aggregateRating.title: aggregateRating
aggregateRating.description: The overall rating of the course, based on a collection of reviews or ratings. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.

citation.title: citation
citation.description: A citation or reference to another creative work, such as another publication or web page, that is relevant to this course. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.

coursePrerequisites.title: coursePrerequisites
coursePrerequisites.description: Requirements for taking the Course. May be completion of another Course, an AlignmentObject, or a textual description like 'Basic Unix skills'. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
coursePrerequisites.$comment: TeSS normalises via markdownify_list. Best practice: list prerequisites clearly so learners can self-assess readiness.

educationalLevel.title: educationalLevel
educationalLevel.description: The level expected to participate in the course. Use 'beginner', 'intermediate', or 'advanced'. Controlled vocabulary: Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
educationalLevel.$comment: Best practice: use 'beginner', 'intermediate', or 'advanced' for maximum interoperability with TeSS and other registries.

hasCourseInstance.title: hasCourseInstance
hasCourseInstance.description: An offering of this course at a specific time and place or through a specific mode of delivery. A course may have multiple instances. Use this to link to CourseInstance objects. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
hasCourseInstance.$comment: Best practice: always provide at least one CourseInstance with courseMode, startDate, and location. TeSS inlines the CourseInstance into the course record. For multiple instances supply an array.

license.title: license
license.description: A license document for this course. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
license.$comment: Best practice: use CC-BY URL or SPDX identifier. Open licensing is strongly encouraged for FAIR training resources.

mentions.title: mentions
mentions.description: Datasets, tools, technologies, entities etc., which are actively used by and/or referred to by this course. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
mentions.$comment: TeSS maps to :external_resources. Use @type 'SoftwareApplication' with bio.tools URLs for software.

provider.title: provider
provider.description: The organization responsible for providing the educational input for the course, e.g. content, assessments, accreditation. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
provider.$comment: TeSS maps to :node_names (provider organisation names) and :content_provider. Best practice: use Organisation objects with @id set to the organisation's persistent URI.

teaches.title: teaches
teaches.description: Statements describing what knowledge, skills or abilities students should acquire on completion of this Course. Controlled vocabulary: Bloom's taxonomy recommended. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.

timeRequired.title: timeRequired
timeRequired.description: Approximate or typical time it takes to work through this course, in ISO 8601 duration format (e.g. 'P3D', 'P1W'). Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
timeRequired.$comment: Use ISO 8601 durations: P3D = 3 days, P1W = 1 week, PT6H = 6 hours.

accessibilitySummary.title: accessibilitySummary
accessibilitySummary.description: A human-readable summary of accessibility features or deficiencies of this course. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

alternateName.title: alternateName
alternateName.description: An alias or alternative name for this course, e.g. a course code. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

audience.title: audience
audience.description: The type of audience intended for this course. A succinct description of the attendees. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

comment.title: comment
comment.description: Comments from users about this course. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

commentCount.title: commentCount
commentCount.description: The number of comments this course has received. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

courseCode.title: courseCode
courseCode.description: The identifier for the course used by the course provider, e.g. 'CS101'. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

creator.title: creator
creator.description: The creator/author of the course content. May differ from the instructor (in CourseInstance) or the provider. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

dateCreated.title: dateCreated
dateCreated.description: The date on which the course was created. ISO 8601 format. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

dateModified.title: dateModified
dateModified.description: The date on which the course was most recently modified. ISO 8601 format. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

educationalCredentialAwarded.title: educationalCredentialAwarded
educationalCredentialAwarded.description: A description of the qualification, award, certificate, or credential awarded on successful completion. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

hasPart.title: hasPart
hasPart.description: Training materials used in the course such as slides or handouts. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

image.title: image
image.description: An image of or representing the course. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

isBasedOn.title: isBasedOn
isBasedOn.description: A course, book or other resource on which this Course is based. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

thumbnailUrl.title: thumbnailUrl
thumbnailUrl.description: A thumbnail image URL relevant to this course. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

examples.name: Applied Genomics
examples.description: A semester-long module introducing applied genomics to life scientists.
teaches: Annotate genomic features using standard tools

CourseInstance.title: CourseInstance
CourseInstance.description: A Bioschemas CourseInstance (v1.0-RELEASE) describes a specific run of a Course at a particular time and place. Schema.org type: CourseInstance (Thing > Event > CourseInstance). Use CourseInstance to capture the when, where, and who of a specific training event. Must be used in tandem with a Course. Profile URL: https://bioschemas.org/profiles/CourseInstance/1.0-RELEASE
CourseInstance.$comment: Minimum required: @context, @type, @id, dct:conformsTo, courseMode, location. Strongly recommended: startDate, endDate, url, instructor (with ORCID), inLanguage, offers. TeSS maps CourseInstance to :events with :event_types = [:workshops_and_courses]. courseMode values 'online'/'onsite' determine the :online boolean in TeSS. Virtual events must include a VirtualLocation with a connection URL.

@type.title: @type
@type.description: Schema.org class. Must be 'CourseInstance'. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: MANY.

@id.title: @id
@id.description: Globally unique IRI identifying this course instance. Should include a run identifier (e.g. year). Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.

courseMode.title: courseMode
courseMode.description: The medium or means of delivery of the course instance. Use 'online', 'onsite', 'hybrid', 'blended', 'synchronous', 'asynchronous', 'full-time', or 'part-time'. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: MANY.
courseMode.$comment: TeSS detects 'online' (case-insensitive) in courseMode values to set :online=true. For virtual events supply 'online'; for in-person supply 'onsite'; for hybrid supply both. See also: https://ceds.ed.gov/element/001311 for standardised values.

location.title: location
location.description: The location of this course instance. For in-person events use Place with PostalAddress. For online events use VirtualLocation with a connection URL. For hybrid events both can be supplied. Marginality: Minimum. Cardinality: ONE.

startDate.title: startDate
startDate.description: The start date and time of this course instance in ISO 8601 format. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
startDate.$comment: Best practice: use full ISO 8601 datetime with timezone (e.g. '2026-09-01T09:00:00Z' or '2026-09-01T09:00:00+01:00'). TeSS stores as :start and can compute :end from :start + :duration if end is absent.

endDate.title: endDate
endDate.description: The end date and time of this course instance in ISO 8601 format. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
endDate.$comment: Best practice: always provide endDate. If absent and duration is present, TeSS can compute it. Provide full ISO 8601 datetime with timezone.

url.title: url
url.description: The preferred URL of this course instance event page. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.

inLanguage.title: inLanguage
inLanguage.description: The language in which the course instance is delivered. Use IETF BCP 47 language codes. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: ONE.
inLanguage.$comment: TeSS extract_language() returns the first subtag before '-'. Use 'en', 'de', 'fr' etc.

instructor.title: instructor
instructor.description: A person assigned to instruct or provide instructional assistance for the CourseInstance. May include main teachers, training assistants, and helpers. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
instructor.$comment: Best practice: include @id with ORCID URI for each instructor. TeSS uses extract_people() to parse ORCID.

offers.title: offers
offers.description: The price an attendee would pay to attend this CourseInstance. Use priceCurrency with ISO 4217 currency code. A price of '0.00' indicates a free event. Marginality: Recommended. Cardinality: MANY.
offers.$comment: Best practice: always include offers even for free events (price: '0.00') to indicate registration information. Use ISO 4217 currency codes: 'GBP', 'EUR', 'USD'.

alternateName.title: alternateName
alternateName.description: An alias or alternative name for this course instance. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

contributor.title: contributor
contributor.description: Secondary contributors to this course instance, e.g. critical reviewers or helpers. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

description.title: description
description.description: A description specific to this CourseInstance. Overrides the Course-level description for this run. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

duration.title: duration
duration.description: The duration of this course instance in ISO 8601 duration format. Overrides Course-level timeRequired for this run. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.
duration.$comment: TeSS can compute :end from :start + :duration when endDate is absent. Use ISO 8601: P2D = 2 days, PT6H = 6 hours.

eventStatus.title: eventStatus
eventStatus.description: The status of this course instance, e.g. 'postponed', 'cancelled', 'registration closed'. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

funder.title: funder
funder.description: A person or organization that financially supports this course instance. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

image.title: image
image.description: An image of or representing this course instance. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

maximumAttendeeCapacity.title: maximumAttendeeCapacity
maximumAttendeeCapacity.description: The maximum number of individuals who may attend. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.

name.title: name
name.description: The name of this specific course instance. Can override the Course name for variation-specific titles. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: ONE.
name.$comment: Use name only when this instance has a different name from the parent Course (e.g. 'Applied Genomics — Autumn 2026 cohort').

organizer.title: organizer
organizer.description: An organizer of this course instance event. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

subEvent.title: subEvent
subEvent.description: An event that is part of this course instance, e.g. a guest lecture within a workshop. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

superEvent.title: superEvent
superEvent.description: An event that this course instance is a part of, e.g. a conference. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.

workFeatured.title: workFeatured
workFeatured.description: The training material used in or produced for this specific course instance. Use to override Course hasPart for instance-specific materials. Marginality: Optional. Cardinality: MANY.
